reason: The mockup presents an elegant, highly cohesive login and entry-point registration screen for the Project Notes web interface. The visual design respects Fitts's Law by ensuring that interactive components like text fields and action buttons have comfortable heights (all at least 48px). The page is highly legible with a strong primary action button ('Sign In') and clear visual hierarchy. Grouping principles (Gestalt) are followed, clearly separating credentials from social authenticators and routing paths. The design operates well within Miller's Law constraints with under 9 distinct visual groups, keeping cognitive load low. WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements are met, particularly with the primary color and dark text on a bright background.
concerns: The social login buttons currently display Google and Apple platforms; if specific authentication backends are not planned, these may represent extraneous visual noise.
Only a single viewport mockup is supplied, preventing validation of complex cross-viewport transformations or responsive behavior on larger screens.
The BottomNavBar is fixed to the bottom, which is standard for mobile layouts, but may feel oversized or out of place on wider desktop monitors.
